description: 12 top football clubs announced breakaway league. Collapsed within 48 hours due to massive backlash from fans, players, politicians, and governing bodies. Tests rapid opinion cascade with near-universal opposition.

key_events.description: Official announcement. JP Morgan backing ($3.5B). UEFA president Ceferin: 'spit in the face of football lovers'.
Boris Johnson threatens legislation. UEFA threatens World Cup/Euros bans. Gary Neville viral monologue.
Chelsea fans block team bus. Premier League captains' joint statement. Players break ranks.
All six English clubs withdraw within hours. Ed Woodward resigns. Domino effect.
Atletico, AC Milan, Inter withdraw. 9 of 12 gone in 48 hours. Florentino Perez: 'on standby'.

calibration_notes: Extreme case of rapid opinion cascade — near-universal opposition in 24-48 hours. Opposition coalition extraordinarily broad (fans, players, rival clubs, governments, FIFA/UEFA, media) with essentially no public constituency supporting it. Fastest collapse of a major sports/commercial initiative in history. Tests: cascade dynamics, coordinated multi-stakeholder backlash, absence of any counterweight constituency.
